ops.title: reposync/synckitd two-host deployment (laptop + yasyf-home) ‚Äî service labels, health checks, deploy flow, restart gotchas

ops.body:
reposync syncs repos across two tailscale-reachable macOS hosts: this laptop (`yasyf@yasyf`, yBook-Pro) and `yasyf@yasyf-home` (both clocks observed in the same local timezone on 2026-07-03; an earlier "yasyf-home logs in IST" note was wrong). As of 2026-07-14 both hosts run reposync 0.15.0 (cask; adds .env file sync, live-verified two-host), cookiesync 0.9.0 (cask), and synckitd (formula, all from `yasyf/tap`).

reposync ‚â•0.14.0 gotchas (learned deploying 0.15.0 on 2026-07-14):
- **`reposync install` must be re-run after upgrading across the 0.14.0 watch-backend flip** (watchman ‚Üí hardened fsnotify): the manifest is only regenerated by install, and a stale watchman manifest cannot watch the individual `.env` file paths 0.15.0 declares (`watchman::RootResolveError: Not a directory` in the serve log; env edits on that host then only propagate via pull/tick). Healthy form for an absent `.env` is an INFO "dir not watchable yet" line.
- **.env watch inode-swap gap (by design)**: a converge that rewrites `.env` (temp+rename) kills that host's file watch until the ~30s fsnotify sweep re-arms it; an edit landing in that window is missed and rides the 15-min tick. Create/edit propagates in ~45-170s.
- **Open investigation** (cc-notes backlog task): deletion-only `.env` rewrites missed the watch fast path in BOTH directions during the 0.15.0 smoke (688s/556s, tick fallback) even with fsnotify manifests on both hosts, while creates/updates went fast ‚Äî not explained by the inode-swap gap alone; correctness unaffected (tick floor + tombstones verified). The mesh registry is pinned to tailscale MagicDNS FQDNs (`yasyf@yasyf.tail71af5d.ts.net` / `yasyf@yasyf-home.tail71af5d.ts.net`) ‚Äî synckit ‚â•0.8.0 mints FQDN targets on `host add`/DetectSelf, so peer ssh rides the tailscale path instead of racing LAN DNS; cookiesync browser-endpoint IDs and `consent_route_to` carry the FQDNs too. The laptop's watchman watches ~139 roots including `cc-review` and `repo-sync`; yasyf-home ~90.

synckitd ‚â•0.8.0 peer-down behavior (verified live via a 3-minute induced tailscale outage): serve's watch path opens a per-peer circuit breaker ‚Äî expect exactly ONE `watch: peer unreachable; suppressing notifies until recovery` warn + ONE `watch: tailscale snapshot` line (online/direct-vs-derp diagnosis) per manifest per outage, raw ssh "Operation timed out" stderr lines at the doubling probe cadence (30s‚Üí5m cap, normal), then ONE `watch: peer recovered ‚Ä¶ down_for=‚Ä¶` on the probe that succeeds (which is itself the full catch-up sync). Converge logs once per outage the same way. The 15-min `synckitd reconcile` tick is a fresh process, so it still emits one unreachable warn per tick while a peer is down ‚Äî by design, not a regression.

Architecture: the external **synckitd** daemon drives consumers (reposync, cookiesync) via manifests at `~/.config/synckit/manifests/*.json` (reposync's: watchman backend, 15s debounce, stdio `rpc-serve`). LaunchAgents per host: `com.github.yasyf.synckit.serve` (resident daemon, socket `~/.config/synckit/rpc.sock`), `com.github.yasyf.synckit.reconcile` (tick), `com.github.yasyf.synckit.helper.cookiesync`. Logs: `~/Library/Logs/synckit/<label>.log`. The old `com.github.yasyf.reposync.*` labels are gone.

Health check: `launchctl list | grep synckit` (serve has a live PID), `ls ~/.config/synckit/rpc.sock`, serve log shows `serving socket=` + `watchbackend: watching dir` lines, `watchman watch-list` includes each tracked repo's `vcs.WatchPaths` leaves.

**Deploy**: tag `vX.Y.Z` + push tag ‚Üí goreleaser publishes to `yasyf/tap` ‚Üí per host `brew upgrade synckitd` / `brew upgrade --cask reposync` ‚Üí `launchctl bootout gui/$(id -u)/com.github.yasyf.synckit.serve` + `bootstrap` the plist (tick needs no restart ‚Äî it execs the stable symlink fresh). Restart gotchas learned 2026-07-02:
- An **orphaned synckitd serve (PPID 1)** can survive `bootout` (it wasn't launchd-managed) and keep the socket/reconcile flock, wedging every new reconcile with "reconcile lock held by another process" ‚Äî `pgrep -fl 'synckitd serve'` and kill strays before bootstrapping.
- A freshly installed binary's **first launchd exec can stall minutes in `state = xpcproxy`** (Gatekeeper scan). Wait it out; don't loop bootout/bootstrap ‚Äî it recovers.
- The watch engine's item set is frozen per daemon generation: after `reposync repo add`, restart serve so watchman subscribes the new repo's leaves.
- **Restart helpers BEFORE serve** (learned 2026-07-03): serve fetches each consumer's capabilities at startup with only a ~60s retry budget; restarting serve while `helper.cookiesync`'s rpc.sock is down leaves that manifest's watches unarmed for the whole generation. Order: bootout/bootstrap helper.cookiesync, wait for its sock, then serve ‚Äî or reload serve once after the helper is up.
- **yasyf-home has no LAN sshd** ‚Äî port 22 is served only by Tailscale SSH (LAN connections get refused, nothing LISTENs in netstat). There is no out-of-band ssh path when tailscale is down on either end; don't take yasyf-home's tailscale down remotely.
- Chrome/Arc cookie DBs live at `<profile>/Network/Cookies` ‚Äî touching the legacy `<profile>/Cookies` path does not generate watch events.
- `helper.cookiesync` logs `WARN Secure Enclave presence unavailable` and falls back to an in-memory key cache when the screen is locked or the session is ssh-only ‚Äî environmental, clears on unlock, not a crash.
- `reposync repo rm <arg>` resolves `<arg>` as a path against the CWD ‚Äî run it from `~/Code` (or pass the absolute clone path), else it "unregisters" a wrong relpath and silently leaves the real entry.

The busy-deferral gate (synckit ‚â•v0.5.0 + reposync ‚â•v0.11.0, [[dont-codify-the-migration]] era work) was verified live 2026-07-02: trunk change under a held `.git/index.lock` ‚Üí serve log `watch: <id>: deferred: git index locked` every 15s, fingerprint unrecorded, repo untouched; `reposync sync` reports `busy (git index locked)`; lock release ‚Üí trunk advanced in ~21s (vs the old 15-min tick).
